results.title: TMRW Closes $7m Seed Round with Tidal Ventures, Partners with ...
results.content:
View profile for Mark Britt

We’ve officially closed our $7m seed round led by Tidal Ventures and inked an exclusive Australian partnership with TruDiagnostic to bring world-leading epigenetic testing to our members.
It was great to chat with Bronwen Clune from Capital Brief recently about why TMRW is taking a different path than most startups. I believe the "building in public" trend is often just an entrepreneur’s ego vision; instead, as we scale, we are committed to building with our patients.
In health, our focus must be on prioritising and optimising them rather than just hitting a "clinical minimum". By using data to increase human connection, we are ensuring our members are truly in charge of their own health journey.

View organization page for Capital Brief [...] View profile for Cameron Priest

Pahoia•15K followers

Really proud of this one.
What Mark Britt, Marko Papuckovski and the whole team are building is very special.
We raised $7M to build TMRW into what we think proactive health should actually look like - not a dashboard with some blood results, but a proper clinical environment built on 1,700+ biomarkers, epigenetic testing through our new exclusive TruDiagnostic partnership, and real human clinicians.
The round was led by Tidal Ventures and Zed Capital, with a bunch of investors who are also TMRW members. That part matters - people backing something they actually use.
Lot of work ahead. Let's go team.

17,843 followers [...] Blood diagnostics have become the bread and butter of a thousand new health platforms. Most will hand you a PDF with 30 data points and call it done. TMRW CEO Mark Britt is building on 1,700.
Longevity play TMRW has raised approximately $7 million in seed funding to expand its clinical footprint and refine patient experience, moving beyond only health tools to a proactive clinical environment.
The round was led by Sydney-based Tidal Ventures, with participation from Melbourne fund Capital Zed and a cohort of high-net-worth entrepreneurs and family offices — the majority of whom are active TMRW members themselves.

results.title: Longevity play TMRW raises $7m seed round from Tidal and inks ...
results.content:
# Longevity play TMRW raises $7m seed round from Tidal and inks TruDiagnostic deal

## The longevity platform founded by former iflix CEO Mark Britt has closed its first VC round — backed, in large part, by its own members

Your cells have an age, and it is not the same as your birthday. Working that out has become a booming industry.

While blood diagnostics have become the bread and butter of a thousand new health platforms, most will hand you a PDF with 30 data points and call it done. TMRW CEO Mark Britt is building on 1,700.

TMRW has raised approximately $7 million in seed funding to expand its clinical footprint and refine the patient experience, moving beyond digital-only health tools to a proactive clinical environment. [...] The round was led by Sydney-based Tidal Ventures, with participation from Melbourne fund Capital Zed and a cohort of high-net-worth entrepreneurs and family offices — the majority of whom are active TMRW members themselves.

results.title: TMRW Raises $7M for Advanced Blood Diagnostics Platform
results.content:
The round was led by Sydney-based Tidal Ventures, with Melbourne fund Capital Zed and a cohort of high-net-worth entrepreneurs and family offices also participating — many of whom are already TMRW members.
Alongside the funding, TMRW has also secured an exclusive Australian partnership with TruDiagnostic, the US-based leader in epigenetic and biological age testing, whose technology is used by longevity advocates Bryan Johnson and Kim Kardashian. [...] This may reshape your expectations of results and actionable outakes from your blood tests. 🩸 ⬇️
Blood diagnostics have become the backbone of a new generation of health platforms. Most hand over a PDF with a few dozen biomarkers and call it insight. TMRW CEO Mark Britt is aiming far higher — building a system that analyses more than 1,700 data points.
The longevity-focused company has raised around $7 million in seed funding to expand its clinical footprint and sharpen the patient experience, positioning itself as a proactive health clinic rather than just another diagnostics platform. [...] 📱 Continuous support via video, chat & digital therapy apps
Focused on multimorbid patients (CKD, diabetes, hypertension), Oska helps translate clinical recommendations into everyday habits—reducing complications and avoidable hospitalisations.
🗣️ “Effective chronic care is driven by daily behaviour, not occasional appointments,” said Niklas Best, CEO & co-founder.
The fresh capital will fuel expansion of Oska’s AI care infrastructure, which has already supported patients for 3+ years.
A strong signal that AI + human care is the future of scalable chronic disease management.
#DigitalHealth #HealthTech #AIinHealthcare #ChronicCare #SeedFunding #EuropeanStartups #PreventiveCare #ValueBasedCare #AIHealth #StartupFunding

Darach Neeson, CEO at SignaCor, says: “The Spark programme’s content was exceptional – value-filled insights from UK life science industry experts and meaningful investor engagement. The lessons learned have influenced our strategy, and we would absolutely recommend it to other founders. We are excited to now join Zinc’s portfolio of innovative life science companies. This funding will allow us to execute our Phase 2a trial plans and advance our regulatory strategy, bringing us closer to delivering a new treatment for patients with limited treatment options.” [...] Agree & Join LinkedIn

# TMRW Raises $4.97M Seed Round Led by Tidal Ventures

View organization page for Startup Researcher Oceania

517 followers

Longevity platform TMRW has raised a US$4.97 million seed round led by Tidal Ventures, with participation from Blackbird Ventures, Archangel Ventures, and Possible Ventures.
Founded by Mark Britt, the company provides personalised longevity programs based on diagnostics like whole genome sequencing and epigenetic testing. TMRW has also announced a partnership with TruDiagnostic to offer its clients advanced biological age testing.
More at: 
#Longevity #HealthTech #Funding #Startup #VentureCapital
